Hi! I'm Víctor Garcés, a full-stack developer from Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic, currently studying Computer Science. With a Technical Degree and over three years of front-end experience creating modern, responsive web experiences, plus nearly a year building back-ends with C#, databases, AWS, Docker and microservices, I cover the whole stack from interface to infrastructure. Beyond coding, I am a passionate filmmaker, photographer, and video editor, skilled in cinematography, storytelling, composition, lighting, and post-production using tools like Adobe Premiere Pro. I merge my technical expertise with my creative vision to deliver visually captivating and functional digital experiences.

Hi! I'm Víctor Garcés, a full-stack developer and filmmaker based in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic. I build responsive, performant front-ends with Vue.js and Nuxt, and back-ends with C#, microservices, Docker and AWS, always prioritizing clean code and user experience.
I craft intuitive digital experiences where design meets functionality. Based in Dominican Republic, bringing ideas and feelings to life through code, pics and creativity.

Answers to common questions about my process and services.

I offer full-stack web development. On the front end: UX/UI design, interactive prototyping, and responsive websites and web applications built with Vue.js/Nuxt.js. On the back end: REST APIs and microservices with C#/.NET, database design, containerization with Docker, and cloud deployments on AWS. My goal is to create seamless digital experiences from concept to deployment.
What technologies do you work with?

**Front-end:** Vue.js, Nuxt, TypeScript and Tailwind CSS. **Back-end:** C#/.NET, SQL databases, microservices architecture, Docker and AWS. I've been building back-end systems professionally for almost a year. **Beyond code:** photography, filmmaking and video editing with Adobe Premiere Pro.

Projects are developed in **phases**: functional deliverables completed sequentially. At the end of each week I present the progress in a short meeting, so you always know where the project stands and can give feedback early. A new phase begins once the previous one is delivered, paid and approved.
What will you need from me?

To keep the project moving I'll need the **content** (copy, images, audio, video), your **design references** (colors, style, logo), a **payment method** to cover the monthly cost of the operation services (hosting, domain and similar), and your feedback in the weekly check-ins. Delays in providing these shift the delivery dates accordingly.
How is a project priced and billed?

Payment is **per phase**: each phase is paid in full at its start, and work on a phase begins once its payment is received. All amounts are in USD. Quotes and timelines correspond to the agreed scope. New features or major changes in requirements are quoted and agreed separately, before any work on them starts.
How long does a project take?

Projects are planned in **weeks**, with an agreed number of dedicated hours per week. Timelines are good-faith estimates and depend on receiving the content, access and approvals on time; delays on those shift the dates accordingly. I reply to questions and requests within up to three business days; a priority support plan is available if you need faster response times.

Each phase includes up to **two rounds of adjustments** on what was agreed for that phase. A phase is considered approved when you confirm it, or after five business days without observations. Extra revisions, or changes to elements that were already approved, are quoted separately.
What about hosting, domains and other services?

I **set up and manage** the services your project needs to run (server, domain, payment processing and similar); their monthly cost is covered by you through a payment method you provide. Payment processing is registered in **your name**, so collections go straight to your account. The initial setup of these services is included within the project phases. Once the project is fully paid, everything delivered is **yours**: files, credentials and accesses included.
Do you offer maintenance and support?

Yes. Once the project is in production, maintenance is available as a **monthly plan**: site availability, security updates, backups and a margin of small adjustments and fixes. For projects that handle payments or user accounts, security maintenance is a required part of the service, not an optional extra. Response times depend on the plan you choose.
What do you enjoy most about your work?

I love the challenge of solving complex problems through design and technology. It's incredibly rewarding to see people interact with something I've created and find it genuinely useful and easy to navigate. Bridging the gap between user needs and technical possibilities is what truly excites me.
What are your hobbies outside of work?

When I'm not coding, I'm usually behind a camera: shooting photography, filming, or editing video. I'm passionate about filmmaking and visual storytelling, and I love exploring Santo Domingo looking for new places and stories to capture.
